skill.name: agent-tools
skill.description: Run 250+ AI apps via inference.sh CLI - image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, 3D, Twitter automation. Models: FLUX, Veo, Gemini, Grok, Claude, Seedance, OmniHuman, Tavily, Exa, OpenRouter, and many more. Use when running AI apps, generating images/videos, calling LLMs, web search, or automating Twitter. Triggers: inference.sh, infsh, ai model, run ai, serverless ai, ai api, flux, veo, claude api, image generation, video generation, openrouter, tavily, exa search, twitter api, grok
skill.summary: Access 250+ AI models through inference.sh CLI for image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, and automation
skill.author: skillssh

security_audit.summary: All 203 static security findings are false positives. The detected patterns are markdown code examples and documentation URLs, not executable code. This skill contains only markdown documentation files with no executable code, scripts, or security risks. The Bash tool restriction to 'infsh *' commands provides additional safety by limiting execution to inference.sh CLI only.

content.value_statement: Execute AI models without local GPUs or complex setup. Access image generation, video creation, LLMs, and search through simple CLI commands that handle authentication and execution automatically.
actual_capabilities: Execute 250+ AI apps via infsh CLI including image generation with FLUX, video creation with Veo, and LLM calls through OpenRouter
Generate sample input JSON schemas for any app using 'infsh app sample' command
Upload local files automatically for processing and receive downloadable URLs for results
Search and filter apps by category or name using 'infsh app list' with various flags
Run tasks asynchronously with '--no-wait' flag and check status later using task IDs
Authenticate via 'infsh login' or INFSH_API_KEY environment variable for CI/CD integration
limitations: Requires internet connection and valid inference.sh account - all processing happens remotely on their servers
CLI tool must be installed separately before use - not embedded in the skill itself
File uploads are limited by network bandwidth and inference.sh account quotas
Cannot execute arbitrary code - restricted to 'infsh *' commands only via allowed-tools configuration

use_cases.title: Generate marketing visuals
use_cases.description: Create product images and promotional videos using FLUX for images and Veo for video generation without managing GPU infrastructure

use_cases.title: Automate content workflows
use_cases.description: Build pipelines that generate images, write captions with LLMs, and post to social media using the Twitter automation apps

use_cases.title: Research and analysis
use_cases.description: Search the web with Tavily, extract relevant data, and synthesize findings using Claude or Gemini models

prompt_templates.title: Generate an image with FLUX
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the inference.sh CLI to generate an image with FLUX. Describe a cat astronaut floating in space with a colorful nebula background. Save the output URL.
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic image generation with specific prompt

prompt_templates.title: Create a video from text prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a video using Google Veo through inference.sh. Create a drone shot flying over a forest at sunset. Use the --no-wait flag so I can check status later.
prompt_templates.scenario: Video generation with async task tracking

prompt_templates.title: Search web and summarize results
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Tavily search via inference.sh to find the latest AI news. Then use Claude Sonnet to summarize the key developments in bullet points.

prompt_templates.title: Process local image file
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an image at /home/user/photo.jpg. Use inference.sh to upscale it 2x using the Topaz upscaler app. Show me the download URL for the result.

output_examples.input: Generate an image of a sunset over mountains using FLUX
output_examples.output:
Running falai/flux-dev-lora
Task ID: abc123def456
Status: completed
Output: https://cloud.inference.sh/app/files/.../sunset.png

output_examples.input: Search for latest AI news with Tavily
output_examples.output:
Running tavily/search-assistant
Task ID: xyz789abc123
Result: Found 5 articles about AI developments including new model releases and research papers from top labs.

output_examples.input: List all available image generation apps
output_examples.output:
Found 42 apps in 'image' category:
- falai/flux-dev-lora: FLUX 2 Dev with LoRA support
- google/gemini-3-pro-image: Gemini 3 Pro Image
- xai/grok-imagine-image: Grok Imagine
...and 39 more

best_practices: Always use 'infsh app sample' to generate input templates before running apps - this ensures correct JSON structure
For long-running tasks like video generation, use the '--no-wait' flag and check status later with 'infsh task get'
Set the INFSH_API_KEY environment variable for CI/CD pipelines instead of interactive login
Use app version pinning (namespace/app-name@version) in production to ensure reproducible results
anti_patterns: Do not pipe arbitrary user input directly into CLI commands - always validate and sanitize input JSON structures
Avoid running multiple apps simultaneously without tracking task IDs - you will lose references to async results
Never hardcode API keys in prompts or scripts - use the INFSH_API_KEY environment variable or infsh login
Do not attempt to execute commands outside the 'infsh *' scope - the skill is restricted to inference.sh CLI only

faq.question: Do I need a GPU to use this skill?
faq.answer: No GPU required. All processing happens on inference.sh cloud servers. You only need the CLI installed and an internet connection.

faq.question: How do I authenticate?
faq.answer: Run 'infsh login' which opens a browser for authentication. For CI/CD, set the INFSH_API_KEY environment variable with your API key.

faq.question: Can I process my local files?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide local file paths in the input JSON and the CLI automatically uploads them. Use absolute paths, relative paths, or paths with home directory.

faq.question: How much does it cost?
faq.answer: Pricing varies by app and usage. Check your inference.sh account balance with 'infsh me'. Each app shows pricing before you run it.

faq.question: What AI models are available?
faq.answer: Over 250 apps including FLUX for images, Veo for video, Claude/Gemini/Grok for text, Tavily/Exa for search, Rodin for 3D, and Twitter automation tools.

faq.question: Can I run tasks in the background?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the '--no-wait' flag to submit tasks and return immediately. Check status later using 'infsh task get <task-id>'.
